basic_details.job_title: Balangir District Court Group C Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 27 Steno, Junior Typist and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Office of the District Judge, Balangir (Balangir District Court)
basic_details.post_name: Junior Clerk-cum-Copyist, Junior Typist, Stenographer Grade-III, Salaried Amin
basic_details.short_information: Office of the District Judge, Balangir is recruiting 27 posts across Junior Clerk-cum-Copyist, Junior Typist, Stenographer Grade-III and Salaried Amin, last date 24 June 2026.

important_dates.exam_date: To be intimated later through post and official website

age_limit.details: Candidates must be above 18 years and below 42 years of age as on the last date of receipt of applications (24-06-2026). Upper age limit relaxation is applicable for reserved category candidates as per relevant State Government Acts, Rules and Orders in force.

qualification.details: Eligibility conditions differ by post. All candidates must be Indian citizens, able to speak, read and write Odia (passed Odia test equivalent to M.E. standard), of good character, sound health and must have not more than one spouse living if married.

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 19900-63200/- (Level-4) to Rs. 25500-81100/- (Level-7) depending on post

selection_process: Part Subject Marks Duration
Part-I (Written Test) English 100 2 Hours
Part-I (Written Test) Arithmetic 100 1 Hour
Part-I (Written Test) General Knowledge 100 1 Hour
Part-II (Practical) Computer Science Test 100 1 Hour
Part-III Viva Voce Test 45 -
Part-I (Written Test) English (Qualifying in nature) 100 2 Hours
Part-II Shorthand & Typewriting Test (Stenographer Grade-III) 50 5 min shorthand + 10 min typewriting
Part-II Typewriting Test (Junior Typist) 50 10 Minutes
Part-III (Practical) Computer Science Test 100 1 Hour
Part-IV Viva Voce Test 35 -
Part-I (Written Test) General English 50 1 Hour
Part-I (Written Test) Technical Knowledge in Survey & Settlement (Theory) 50 30 Minutes
Part-I (Written Test) Handwriting in Odia 50 30 Minutes
Part-II (Practical) Skill Test in Computer Application 50 45 Minutes
Part-II (Practical) Technical Knowledge in Survey & Settlement (Practical) 50 45 Minutes
Part-III Viva Voce Test 35 -
Selection will be based on Written Test
Practical Test and Viva Voce in stages. Candidates must clear each stage to proceed to the next. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the recruitment examination.
For the Post of Junior Clerk-cum-Copyist
For the Post of Stenographer Grade-III and Junior Typist
For the Post of Salaried Amin

syllabus.details: The syllabus for each post is defined in the official notification. Below is a quick subject-wise summary to help candidates start preparation immediately.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted offline only; no online applications will be accepted. Candidates must fill in Form-A (prescribed format given in the notification) in their own handwriting with full signature and attach all required documents. Separate applications must be submitted for each post applied for.
Documents Required for Junior Clerk-cum-Copyist, Junior Typist and Stenographer Grade-III:
Self-attested copies of +3 / Graduation certificate and mark sheet
Self-attested copies of +2 certificate and mark sheet
Self-attested copies of HSC (10th) certificate and mark sheet
Self-attested copy of Diploma in Computer Application certificate and mark sheet
Self-attested copy of technical certificate (for Stenographer Grade-III and Junior Typist)
Self-attested copy of Disability Certificate (for PwD candidates only)
Self-attested copy of Caste Certificate (for reserved category candidates)
Self-attested copy of Employment Exchange Registration Certificate
Self-attested copy of Odia pass certificate (if Odia not studied in course)
Self-attested copy of ID card/document (for Sports persons/Ex-Servicemen)
Self-declaration regarding marital status (one spouse living)
Undertaking regarding no criminal antecedent
Three self-addressed envelopes with Rs. 60/- postage stamp affixed on each
Three self-attested recent passport-size photographs (one affixed on application form)
Two original character certificates from two separate Gazetted Officers/Medical Practitioners/Sarpanch
No Objection Certificate from controlling authority (for Government servants and PSU employees)
Documents Required for Salaried Amin:
Self-attested copy of HSC (10th) or equivalent certificate
Self-attested copy of HSC mark sheet
Self-attested copy of Diploma in Computer Application certificate
Self-attested copy of Revenue Inspector Training certificate
Self-attested copy of Employment Exchange Registration Certificate
Self-attested copy of Caste Certificate (for reserved category candidates)
Self-attested copy of Disability Certificate (for physically handicapped candidates only)
Self-attested copy of Odia pass certificate (if Odia not studied in course)
Self-attested copy of certificate for special reserved category (if applicable)
Self-attested ID card/document (for Sports persons/Ex-Servicemen)
Self-declaration regarding marital status (one spouse living)
Undertaking regarding no criminal antecedent
Three self-addressed envelopes with Rs. 60/- postage stamp affixed on each
Three self-attested recent passport-size photographs (one affixed on application form)
Two original character certificates from two separate Gazetted Officers/Medical Practitioners/Sarpanch
No Objection Certificate from controlling authority (for Government servants and PSU employees)
Send Application To: The District Judge, Balangir, At – Civil Courts, Balangir (Patnagarh Road), Po/Dist – Balangir, Pin – 767001

Applications can also be dropped in the box kept inside the Civil Court Premises, Balangir (in front of the Help Desk) during office hours on working days. For updates visit: https://balangir.dcourts.gov.in
important_instructions: The post name must be written in CAPITAL LETTERS on top of the application form and on the envelope. Separate applications with separate sets of documents must be submitted for each post applied for. Written examinations for all posts will be held on the same day; candidates applying for more than one post can appear for only one post's exam of their choice. No examination fee is required from any candidate. Applications received after 5:00 PM on 24-06-2026 or through any mode other than Registered Post/Speed Post/Drop Box will be rejected outright. Non-compliance with any requirement mentioned in the advertisement, including non-submission of any required document, will result in rejection of the application without any correspondence. Fraudulent certificates will lead to prosecution of the candidate even after joining. No TA/DA will be admissible to candidates for attending the recruitment examination. The District Judge, Balangir reserves the right to modify, revoke or cancel the advertisement/recruitment process at any time without prior notice or assigning any reason. The decision of the District Judge, Balangir shall be final as regards the entire recruitment process. Government servants and PSU employees must apply through proper channel with a No Objection Certificate. The engagement of Salaried Amin post is subject to the outcome of W.P.(C) No. 1273/2014 pending before the Hon'ble High Court of Orissa. Candidates are advised to regularly visit the official website https://balangir.dcourts.gov.in for updates on examination dates and other notifications.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Balangir District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application form for Balangir District Court Recruitment 2026 is 24 June 2026. Candidates must ensure that the application reaches the Office of the District Judge, Balangir before the deadline through Registered Post, Speed Post, or Drop Box.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in Balangir District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 27 vacancies have been announced under Group ‘C’ category posts. The recruitment includes Junior Clerk-cum-Copyist, Junior Typist, Stenographer Grade-III, and Salaried Amin posts in the Judgeship of Balangir, Odisha.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for Balangir District Court Jobs 2026?
faq.answer: The application process is completely offline. Candidates must submit the prescribed application form either through Registered Post, Speed Post, or by dropping it in the designated Drop Box at the Office of the District Judge, Balangir.

faq.question: Q4. What is the selection process for Balangir District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a Written Examination, Practical Test, and Viva Voce Test. Candidates applying for Typist and Stenographer posts must also qualify in the required skill tests as per the recruitment rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered under Balangir District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive salary under Level-4 to Level-7 pay matrix. The pay scale ranges from Rs. 19,900/- to Rs. 81,100/- depending on the post and applicable government rules.

faq.question: Q6. Which posts are available in Balangir District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The recruitment includes four Group ‘C’ posts: Junior Clerk-cum-Copyist, Junior Typist, Stenographer Grade-III, and Salaried Amin. Vacancy distribution for each post is available in the official notification PDF.

faq.question: Q7. Where can candidates download the Balangir District Court Notification PDF 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can download the official notification PDF from the official website of the District Court Balangir at Balangir District Court Official Website. The notification contains eligibility, syllabus, exam pattern, and application format details.

faq.question: Q8. What is the job location for Balangir District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will be appointed under the Judgeship of Balangir, Odisha. All postings and related duties will be within the jurisdiction of the Balangir District Court establishment.
